questions.q: Explain the impact of colonialism on the Indian countryside. How did it affect the Indian peasantry and the economy of rural India? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The impact of colonialism on the Indian countryside was devastating. The introduction of the Permanent Settlement of 1793 and the Ryotwari and Mahalwari systems led to the concentration of land in the hands of a few big landlords and moneylenders. This resulted in the exploitation of the Indian peasantry, who were forced to pay heavy rents and interest on their loans. The economy of rural India was also severely affected as the Indian peasants were forced to produce cash crops such as cotton and indigo for the British market, leading to a decline in food production and a rise in prices. This led to widespread poverty and starvation in rural India.
marking_scheme: Understanding of the impact of colonialism on the Indian countryside (1 mark)
Explanation of the effects on the Indian peasantry (1 mark)
Analysis of the impact on the economy of rural India (1 mark)
Use of specific examples to support the answer (1 mark)
Conclusion summarizing the impact of colonialism (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the main features of the Permanent Settlement of 1793 and the Ryotwari and Mahalwari systems. How did they affect the Indian peasants and the economy of rural India? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The Permanent Settlement of 1793, introduced by Lord Cornwallis, was a policy that allowed the British East India Company to collect revenue from Indian peasants directly. The Ryotwari system, introduced in the 19th century, allowed the British to collect revenue from individual peasants directly, while the Mahalwari system, introduced in the same century, allowed the British to collect revenue from villages as a whole. Both systems led to the concentration of land in the hands of a few big landlords and moneylenders, who exploited the Indian peasants by charging them high rents and interest on loans. This led to widespread poverty and starvation in rural India.
marking_scheme: Understanding of the main features of the Permanent Settlement of 1793 (1 mark)
Explanation of the Ryotwari and Mahalwari systems (1 mark)
Analysis of the impact on the Indian peasants (1 mark)
Analysis of the impact on the economy of rural India (1 mark)
Use of specific examples to support the answer (1 mark)

questions.q: What were the effects of the British colonial policies on the Indian economy? How did they lead to the decline of the Indian cotton industry? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The British colonial policies had a devastating impact on the Indian economy. The introduction of the free trade policy led to the flooding of Indian markets with cheap British goods, leading to a decline in domestic industries such as the Indian cotton industry. The Indian cotton industry, which was a major sector in the Indian economy, was forced to compete with the cheap British cotton, leading to its decline. The British also imposed heavy taxes on Indian exports, leading to a decline in India's international trade.
marking_scheme: Understanding of the effects of British colonial policies on the Indian economy (1 mark)
Explanation of the decline of the Indian cotton industry (1 mark)
Analysis of the impact on the Indian economy (1 mark)
Use of specific examples to support the answer (1 mark)
Conclusion summarizing the effects of British colonial policies (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the average yield of wheat per acre in a village that produces 3000 quintals of wheat per year from 100 acres of land. If 20% of the land is irrigated, what is the average yield of wheat per acre in the irrigated and non-irrigated areas? (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation: Average yield of wheat per acre = Total production / Total area of land
Average yield of wheat per acre = 3000 quintals / 100 acres
Average yield of wheat per acre = 30 quintals per acre
Since 20% of the land is irrigated, the average yield of wheat per acre in the irrigated area is 30 quintals per acre, while the average yield of wheat per acre in the non-irrigated area is 25 quintals per acre.
marking_scheme: Calculation of the average yield of wheat per acre (2 marks)
Separate calculation of the average yield of wheat per acre in the irrigated and non-irrigated areas (2 marks)
Use of correct formula and units (1 mark)

questions.q: A farmer produces 500 quintals of wheat from 50 acres of land. If the yield of wheat per acre is 10 quintals per acre, what is the total revenue earned by the farmer from the sale of wheat, assuming the price of wheat is Rs. 100 per quintal? (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation: Total revenue = Total production x Price per quintal
Total revenue = 500 quintals x Rs. 100 per quintal
Total revenue = Rs. 50,000
marking_scheme: Calculation of the total revenue (2 marks)
Use of correct formula and units (2 marks)
Assumption of correct price per quintal (1 mark)

questions.q: A village produces 2000 quintals of cotton per year from 100 acres of land. If the yield of cotton per acre is 20 quintals per acre, and the price of cotton is Rs. 80 per quintal, what is the total revenue earned by the farmer from the sale of cotton? (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation: Total revenue = Total production x Price per quintal
Total revenue = 2000 quintals x Rs. 80 per quintal
Total revenue = Rs. 1,60,000
marking_scheme: Calculation of the total revenue (2 marks)
Use of correct formula and units (2 marks)
Assumption of correct price per quintal (1 mark)
